---
title: "resume"
format: html
---

                                      DAVID T. WATKEYS
Roanoke, VA  •  (419) 799-1637  •  davwat22@vt.edu  •  coldwarcollateraldamage.com

History graduate student and U.S. Navy veteran specializing in Indigenous sovereignty, the history of the war on drugs, legal history, and twentieth-century U.S. environmental and cultural history. Experienced in leadership, consultation, archival research, scholarly editing, database construction, accesibility software and developing public-facing historical communication. 

                                         EDUCATION 
Master of Arts in History (In Progress)	
Virginia Tech — Blacksburg, VA  |  GPA: 3.91  |  Funded Fellowship  |                  Expected May 2026
Bachelor of Arts in History	2024

Virginia Tech — Blacksburg, VA  |  GPA: 3.787  |  Magna Cum Laude

Associate of Science	2022
Virginia Western Community College — Roanoke, VA  |  GPA: 3.756  |  Magna Cum Laude

                                  PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE 
Research & Exhibit Development Consultant | Salem Museum – Salem, VA                         Jun-Aug 2025
•	Collaborated with archaeologists and museum staff to research and develop a 1,500 sq. ft. ++physical exhibit documenting Tutelo and Monacan cultural artifacts recovered from a 1990s excavation of the Moyer softball fields
•	Contributed to a parallel exhibit presenting a timeline of Indigenous history in the Roanoke Valley region

Research Assistant  |  Virginia Tech Library  —  Blacksburg, VA	                            2024–Present
•	Built a searchable primary-source database of approximately 250 datafied sources to support faculty and student research at Virginia Tech, in collaboration with Professor Bruce Pencek

Graduate Editor |  Virginia Tech Undergraduate Historical Review	                          2024–Present
•	Edited and shepherded five articles through peer-reviewed undergraduate journal publication, with four more in production
•	Recruited and led a team of eight undergraduate and one graduate editorial assistant

Administrative Assistant to Two-Star Admiral  |  U.S. Navy (NAVELSG)  —  Williamsburg, VA	2011–2015
•	Supported flag-level command administration; awarded Junior Sailor of the Year (2012)
•	Honorably discharged; eligible for federal employment veteran's preference

                                TEACHING & EDITORIAL MENTORSHIP 
Senior Graduate Editor / Mentor  |  Virginia Tech Undergraduate Historical Review	2024–Present
•	Supervised and mentored a team of eight undergraduates and one graduate student in scholarly editing and academic writing
•	Developed and graded assessments based on course content; specializes in evaluation of written historical argument
Teaching Intern  |  Woodrow Wilson Middle School  —  Roanoke, VA	
•	Completed a six-month classroom internship in secondary history education

                                 RESEARCH & DIGITAL PROJECTS 
Cold War Collateral Damage: A Navajo Chronicle  (Undergraduate Thesis / Independent Study with Dr. Ed Gitre, Virginia Tech, 2024)
•	Designed and published a WordPress digital history site documenting Navajo displacement and uranium contamination during the Cold War era watkeyswordpress.vt.domains/LandingZone/
CONFERENCE & PROFESSIONAL SERVICE 
Lead Coordinator |  Bryan Bertoti Innovative Perspectives in History Conference  —  Virginia Tech	2026
•	Serving as lead organizer for the annual graduate history conference at Virginia Tech
Conference Staff  |  Bryan Bertoti Innovative Perspectives in History Conference  —  Virginia Tech	2025
•	Contributed to behind-the-scenes conference planning and logistics

                                      SKILLS & LANGUAGES 
Technical:  R (in progress)  •  WordPress  •  ADA-compliant PDF production (PREP software)  •  Primary-source database construction
Languages:  English (native)  •  Spanish (reading proficiency)

                                      HONORS & AWARDS 
Funded M.A. Fellowship, Virginia Tech History Dept. (2024–2026)  •  Gallagher Award, Virginia Tech (2023)  •  Curtis Award, Virginia Tech (2023)  •  Environmental History Scholarship, Virginia Tech (2023)  •  Magna Cum Laude, Virginia Tech (2024)  •  Magna Cum Laude, VWCC (2022)  •  Tau Sigma Honor Society)  •  Junior Sailor of the Year (2012)

                                      RESEARCH INTERESTS
Education and Public Outreach   •  Indigenous History  •  Legal & Constitutional History  •  Environmental History  •  Nuclear History  •  Veterans' Histories  •  The War on Drugs  •  Twentieth-Century U.S. History  •  Public History & Digital Humanities
